mission.title: Install and Configure Cedar on Kubernetes
mission.description: Cedar is a language for writing and enforcing authorization policies in your applications. By installing Cedar, you can leverage its expressive and performant capabilities to manage fine-grained permissions effectively.

steps.title: Add the Cedar Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Cedar Helm repository to your local Helm configuration to access the Cedar charts.
```bash
helm repo add cedar https://charts.cedarpolicy.com
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Cedar using Helm
steps.description:
Install Cedar in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm. This command will create a new namespace called 'cedar' and deploy Cedar in it.
```bash
helm install cedar cedar/cedar --namespace cedar --create-namespace --version v4.9.1
```

steps.title: Verify the Cedar installation
steps.description:
Check if the Cedar pods are running successfully in the 'cedar' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cedar
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Cedar
steps.description:
Edit the Cedar deployment to set resource limits for better performance management.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment cedar --namespace cedar
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     memory: "512Mi"
#     cpu: "500m"
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Cedar
steps.description:
Create a Role and RoleBinding to allow access to Cedar's resources.
```bash
kubectl create role cedar-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods --namespace cedar
kubectl create rolebinding cedar-role-binding --role=cedar-role --serviceaccount=default:default --namespace cedar
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Cedar will show the Cedar pods running in the 'cedar' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and RBAC for secure access management.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add cedar https://charts.cedarpolicy.com
helm repo update
helm install cedar cedar/cedar --namespace cedar --create-namespace --version v4.9.1

kubectl edit deployment cedar --namespace cedar
# Add the following under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     memory: "512Mi"
#     cpu: "500m"

kubectl create role cedar-role --verb=get,list,watch --resource=pods --namespace cedar
kubectl create rolebinding cedar-role-binding --role=cedar-role --serviceaccount=default:default --namespace cedar

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description: Uninstall the Cedar release from your Kubernetes cluster using Helm. This will remove the deployment and associated resources created by the Helm chart.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description: Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volumes that were created during the installation. This ensures that all resources are cleaned up.

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description: Delete the 'cedar' namespace to remove any remaining resources. Verify that the namespace has been deleted successfully.

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Cedar installation. You can use the following command to export the current configuration.

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description: Update the Cedar Helm repository and run the upgrade command to upgrade Cedar to the latest version.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description: Check if the Cedar pods are running the new version successfully in the 'cedar' namespace after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description: If the Cedar pods are stuck in CrashLoopBackOff, check the logs for errors. Use the following command to view the logs of a specific pod.

troubleshooting.title: Cedar service not responding
troubleshooting.description: If the Cedar service is not responding, ensure that the service is correctly configured and that the pods are running. Use the following command to check the service status.

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permissions issues
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter permission denied errors, verify that the RBAC roles and bindings are correctly set up. Use the following command to list the roles in the 'cedar' namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Resource limits causing OOMKilled
troubleshooting.description: If pods are being killed due to out of memory (OOMKilled), consider adjusting the resource limits set for Cedar. Use the following command to edit the deployment.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
